title: Quiz: what a transaction guarantees
prompt_md: You wrap two `UPDATE` statements inside a single transaction (`BEGIN ... COMMIT`). What does this guarantee?
options: The two updates run faster than they would separately
Either both updates apply, or neither does — they can't be left half-done
Other users are permanently locked out of the database until you commit
The updates are automatically retried forever until they succeed
explanation_md: This is atomicity — the 'A' in ACID. A transaction is all-or-nothing: if anything fails before `COMMIT`, the whole group rolls back as if it never happened, so you can't end up with one update applied and the other lost. Transactions are about correctness, not speed; they don't lock everyone out permanently, and they don't auto-retry on their own.
